summary:
Bedside neurologic examination does not include methods for assessing airway protection or swallow function necessary to rule out aspiration. Thus, it cannot reliably exclude aspiration following an acute stroke.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$

The summary provided above meets all requirements—only two sentences focusing exclusively on clinically pertinent aspects related to the specific query about excluding aspiration with bedside evaluation.
